front: What is the primary purpose of a Burndown Chart in Agile?
back: A Burndown Chart is a graphical representation of work left to do versus time. It helps the team track progress and predict when all work will be completed, showing the total effort against the amount of work for each iteration.

front: Define a User Story in the context of Agile development.
back: A User Story is a tool used in Agile to capture a description of a software feature from an end-user perspective. It follows a simple template: 'As a [type of user], I want [some goal] so that [some reason].' It helps shift the focus from writing requirements to discussing them.

front: What is the main function of a Kanban Board?
back: A Kanban Board is a visual workflow management tool that helps teams visualize their work, limit work-in-progress (WIP), and maximize efficiency (or flow). Work items are represented as cards that move through columns representing stages of the development process.

front: What are the three standard questions answered during a Daily Stand-up (Scrum) meeting?
back: 1. What did I accomplish yesterday? 2. What will I work on today? 3. What obstacles are impeding my progress? The goal is to synchronize the team's activities and create a plan for the next 24 hours, not to provide detailed status reports.

front: What is the primary difference between a Product Backlog and a Sprint Backlog?
back: The Product Backlog is a prioritized list of everything that might be needed in the product, maintained by the Product Owner. The Sprint Backlog is the set of Product Backlog items selected for the current Sprint, plus a plan for delivering them, created by the Development Team.
